title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
description: Sell services to individuals or businesses. May describe options or resolve client problems.

profiles.description: Answer customers' questions about services, prices, availability, or credit terms.
profiles.rationale: AI can draft and retrieve answers to “customers’ questions about services, prices, availability, or credit terms,” but a rep typically handles live nuances and exceptions, and ICMI contact-center benchmarks put average customer inquiry interactions around 6–10 minutes.

profiles.description: Attend sales or trade meetings or read related publications to obtain information about market conditions, business trends, regulations, or industry developments.
profiles.rationale: AI can summarize “sales or trade meetings” content and “related publications,” but humans still attend and interpret implications, and Salesforce State of Sales/Gartner benchmarks suggest roughly an hour a week on market learning and enablement.

profiles.description: Compute and compare costs of services.
profiles.rationale: Config/price tools let an agent automatically “compute and compare costs of services,” and Salesforce CPQ/operations benchmarks indicate manual pricing comps typically take ~15–30 minutes without automation.

profiles.description: Consult with clients after sales or contract signings to resolve problems and provide ongoing support.
profiles.rationale: “Consult with clients after sales… to resolve problems and provide ongoing support” requires sensitive judgment and relationship management best handled by a person, and Gartner/ICMI data show complex B2B support conversations often run 15–20 minutes.

profiles.description: Contact prospective or existing customers to discuss how services can meet their needs.
profiles.rationale: “Contact prospective or existing customers to discuss how services can meet their needs” is a persuasive, rapport‑building conversation requiring human judgment, with Gartner inside‑sales talk time benchmarks around 10–15 minutes per connect.

profiles.description: Create forms or agreements to complete sales.
profiles.rationale: Standardized templates let AI “create forms or agreements to complete sales” end‑to‑end in CLM/CPQ workflows, whereas SiriusDecisions/Forrester sales‑ops benchmarks show assembling deal paperwork manually often takes ~20–45 minutes.

profiles.description: Develop sales presentations or proposals to explain service specifications.
profiles.rationale: AI can draft “sales presentations or proposals to explain service specifications,” but reps refine messaging and approvals, and Gartner/Salesforce benchmarks indicate building tailored proposals typically takes 1–2 hours.

profiles.description: Distribute promotional materials at meetings, conferences, or trade shows.
profiles.rationale: “Distribute promotional materials at meetings, conferences, or trade shows” requires physical presence and interpersonal interaction, and BLS ATUS/Salesforce State of Sales suggest about an hour per week on event/outreach activities when averaged over time.

profiles.description: Emphasize or recommend service features based on knowledge of customers' needs and vendor capabilities and limitations.
profiles.rationale: AI can suggest options to “emphasize or recommend service features based on… customers’ needs and vendor capabilities,” but a seller tailors and delivers the message, with Gartner pre‑call planning/solutioning often ~30 minutes per opportunity.

profiles.description: Identify prospective customers using business directories, leads from clients, or information from conferences or trade shows.
profiles.rationale: An agent can automatically “identify prospective customers using business directories… or information from conferences,” and TOPO/SiriusDecisions SDR research benchmarks show manual lead research/enrichment takes ~6–10 minutes per lead.

profiles.description: Inform customers of contracts or other information pertaining to purchased services.
profiles.rationale: Automations can “inform customers of contracts or other information pertaining to purchased services” via templated emails/portals, and Salesforce admin productivity data place such follow‑ups at ~5–10 minutes when done manually.

profiles.description: Maintain customer records using automated systems.
profiles.rationale: Systems can auto‑update to “maintain customer records using automated systems,” and Salesforce State of Sales indicates individual CRM updates take ~3–5 minutes each when done by hand.

profiles.description: Monitor market conditions, innovations, and competitors' services, prices, and sales.
profiles.rationale: AI can track and summarize feeds to “monitor market conditions, innovations, and competitors’ services, prices, and sales,” while humans interpret and act, with Gartner competitive‑intelligence routines often budgeting ~1 hour weekly.

profiles.description: Negotiate prices or terms of sales or service agreements.
profiles.rationale: “Negotiate prices or terms of sales or service agreements” entails legal/commercial risk and final authority that must remain human, and Gartner/SiriusDecisions benchmarks show negotiation meetings/discussions often last 30–60 minutes.

profiles.description: Quote prices, credit terms, contract terms, or fulfillment dates for services.
profiles.rationale: CPQ and availability tools allow automated “quote prices, credit terms, contract terms, or fulfillment dates for services,” and sales‑ops benchmarks put manual quoting at ~10–20 minutes per quote.
